Title: Website Does Not Use HTTPS
Evidence: The target website is using HTTP instead of HTTPS.
Description: The target website is using HTTP instead of HTTPS.
Recommendation: Use HTTPS with a valid TLS/SSL certificate.

Title: Missing Security Header: Strict-Transport-Security
Evidence: HSTS header is missing. This helps enforce HTTPS connections.
Description: HSTS header is missing. This helps enforce HTTPS connections.

Title: Missing Security Header: Content-Security-Policy
Evidence: Content Security Policy is missing. This may increase XSS risk.
Description: Content Security Policy is missing. This may increase XSS risk.

Title: Missing Security Header: X-Frame-Options
Evidence: X-Frame-Options header is missing. The site may be vulnerable to clickjacking.
Description: X-Frame-Options header is missing. The site may be vulnerable to clickjacking.

Title: Missing Security Header: Permissions-Policy
Recommendation: Add Permissions-Policy to limit browser feature access.

Title: Information Disclosure in Header: Server
Description: The response exposes server technology information: Server: CyberScanWeb/1.0 Python/3.13.2
Recommendation: Remove or minimize technology/version information from HTTP response headers.

Title: Form Uses GET Method: Form #1
Description: A form uses the GET method. Sensitive data may appear in URLs.
Recommendation: Use POST for forms that send sensitive information.

Title: Password Form Without HTTPS: Form #1
Description: A password field was found on a non-HTTPS page.
Recommendation: Always use HTTPS for login and password forms.

Title: Sensitive Input Allows Browser Autocomplete: Form #1
Description: The input 'password' may allow browser autocomplete for sensitive data.
Recommendation: Disable autocomplete for sensitive inputs or use context-appropriate autocomplete values.

Title: Possible Error Message Disclosure
Description: The page contains possible technical error text: stack trace
Recommendation: Disable detailed error messages in production.

Title: SQL Injection Review Candidate: Form #1
Description: The form contains fields commonly connected to database lookup or authentication logic.
Recommendation: Validate input server-side and use parameterized queries for all database access.

Title: Possible DOM XSS Source-to-Sink Pattern
Evidence: Sources: location.hash; sinks: innerHTML, insertAdjacentHTML, setTimeout(, setInterval(
Description: Inline JavaScript appears to read browser-controlled input and write to risky HTML/script sinks.
Recommendation: Use safe DOM APIs such as textContent, sanitize HTML with a proven sanitizer, and avoid eval-like execution.

Title: Stored XSS Review Candidate
Description: The page contains user-content style inputs that may later be rendered back to other users.
Recommendation: HTML-encode stored user content by output context, sanitize rich text, and add regression tests for stored XSS.

Title: Password Field Missing Explicit Autocomplete Context
Description: A password input does not declare current-password or new-password autocomplete context.
Recommendation: Use autocomplete=current-password for login forms and autocomplete=new-password for registration or reset forms.

Title: Client-Side Authentication Surface Detected
Description: The page or bundled script references authentication tokens, login, registration, or password flows.
Recommendation: Review token storage, password reset protections, lockout/rate limiting, and session expiration server-side.
